channel.title: Nature Communications
channel.description:
Nature Communications is an open access, multidisciplinary journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research in all areas of the biological, health, physical, chemical and Earth sciences. Papers published by the journal aim to represent important advances of significance to specialists within each field.

We are committed to providing an efficient service for both authors and readers. Our team of independent editors make rapid and fair publication decisions. Prompt dissemination of accepted papers to a wide readership and beyond is achieved through a programme of continuous online publication.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Author Correction: Operando microimaging of crystal structure and orientation in all components of all-solid-state-batteries
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 06 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-69145-1

Author Correction: Operando microimaging of crystal structure and orientation in all components of all-solid-state-batteries
item.dc:title: Author Correction: Operando microimaging of crystal structure and orientation in all components of all-solid-state-batteries
item.dc:creator: Quentin Jacquet
Jacopo Cele
Lara Casiez
Samuel Tardif
Asma Medjahed
Stephanie Pouget
Manfred Burghammer
Sandrine Lyonnard
Sami Oukassi

item.title: Enhancing film bulk acoustic resonators performance by optimizing AlN seed layer crystallinity and polarity alignment
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 06 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-69096-7

A single-crystalline aluminum nitride seed layer improves crystal alignment in scandium-doped aluminum nitride films, and subsequent removal eliminates polarity mismatch, enabling high-performance film bulk acoustic resonators and wideband filters.
item.dc:title: Enhancing film bulk acoustic resonators performance by optimizing AlN seed layer crystallinity and polarity alignment
item.dc:creator: Tingting Yang
Qinwen Xu
Yaxin Wang
Yao Cai
Haiyang Li
Yupeng Zheng
Min Zeng
Jingyi Qu
Lei Li
Chao Gao
Xiyu Gu
Binghui Lin
Shishang Guo
Cheng Lei
Sheng Liu
Chengliang Sun

item.title: Observation of mechanical kink control and generation via acoustic waves
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 06 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-68688-7

Kinks are topological solitons and central to many physical systems. Here, the authors experimentally demonstrate control and generation of zero-energy kinks in a topological mechanical metamaterial via acoustic waves and numerically show rich acoustic-wavekink dynamics in highly discrete systems.
item.dc:title: Observation of mechanical kink control and generation via acoustic waves
item.dc:creator: Kai Qian
Nan Cheng
Francesco Serafin
Nicolas Herard
Kai Sun
Georgios Theocharis
Xiaoming Mao
Nicholas Boechler
